title: Getting Started with nwaa
text: nwaa package provides R interface USGS National Water Availability Assessment (NWAA) Data Companion web service (https://water.usgs.gov/nwaa-data). service publishes model outputs HUC12 spatial resolution across three families:
code: library(nwaa)

title: Getting Started with nwaa
text: Every supported model variables live single tibble: list variables one model:
code: nwaa_catalog() #> # A tibble: 8 × 9 #>   model_id                          model_label     family start_ym end_ym temporal variables variable_name units #>   <chr>                             <chr>           <chr>  <chr>    <chr>  <list>   <list>    <list>        <lis> #> 1 wu-irrigation-cu                  Crop Irrigatio… wu     2000-01  2020-… <chr>    <chr [1]> <chr [1]>     <chr> #> 2 wu-irrigation-wd                  Crop Irrigatio… wu     2000-01  2020-… <chr>    <chr [3]> <chr [3]>     <chr> #> 3 wu-public-supply-cu               Public Supply … wu     2009-01  2020-… <chr>    <chr [1]> <chr [1]>     <chr> #> 4 wu-public-supply-wd               Public Supply … wu     2000-01  2020-… <chr>    <chr [3]> <chr [3]>     <chr> #> 5 wu-thermoelectric                 Thermoelectric… wu     2008-01  2020-… <chr>    <chr [7]> <chr [7]>     <chr> #> 6 wqn-conus404-ba                   Atmospheric Fo… wqn    1979-10  2021-… <chr>    <chr [1]> <chr [1]>     <chr> #> 7 wqn-ensemble-conus-nwaa-v1        Hydrologic Mod… wqn    2009-10  2020-… <chr>    <chr [6]> <chr [6]>     <chr> #> 8 iwa-assessment-outputs-conus-2025 National Water… iwa    2009-10  2020-… <chr>    <chr [4]> <chr [4]>     <chr> catalog <- nwaa_catalog() catalog$variables[catalog$model_id == "wu-irrigation-conus-nwaa-v1"][[1]] #> Error in catalog$variables[catalog$model_id == "wu-irrigation-conus-nwaa-v1"][[1]]: subscript out of bounds

title: Getting Started with nwaa
text: NWAA data published HUC12 resolution, service accepts queries HUC length, state, county FIPS. package resolves : State county codes searchable:
code: nwaa_location_types() #> # A tibble: 8 × 2 #>   type     notes                                                                                                  #>   <chr>    <chr>                                                                                                  #> 1 huc2     Hydrologic unit code (2 digits). Returns HUC12 results within this HUC.                                #> 2 huc4     Hydrologic unit code (4 digits). Returns HUC12 results within this HUC.                                #> 3 huc6     Hydrologic unit code (6 digits). Returns HUC12 results within this HUC.                                #> 4 huc8     Hydrologic unit code (8 digits). Returns HUC12 results within this HUC.                                #> 5 huc10    Hydrologic unit code (10 digits). Returns HUC12 results within this HUC.                               #> 6 huc12    Hydrologic unit code (12 digits). Returns results for this HUC12.                                      #> 7 statecd  State abbreviation (2-letter), lowercase in the API (example: 'ca', 'al').Use intersection to control… #> 8 countycd County code (5-digit). Use intersection to control which HUC12s get included. head(nwaa_state_codes()) #> # A tibble: 6 × 2 #>   state_name state_abbr #>   <chr>      <chr>      #> 1 Alabama    AL         #> 2 Alaska     AK         #> 3 Arizona    AZ         #> 4 Arkansas   AR         #> 5 California CA         #> 6 Colorado   CO

title: Getting Started with nwaa
text: three family wrappers share argument structure: pick location, pick variables, pick date range, optionally pick time_res ("monthly", "annualwy", "annualcy"). returns tibble.

title: Getting Started with nwaa
code: wu <- nwaa_water_use(   model_id     = "wu-irrigation-conus-nwaa-v1",   variable_ids = "tot_irr",   location_id  = "18030012",   range        = "custom",   start        = "2020-01",   end          = "2020-12",   time_res     = "monthly" ) head(wu)

title: Getting Started with nwaa
code: atmos <- nwaa_atmos(   variable_ids = "precip",   location_id  = "18030012",   range        = "custom",   start        = "2020-01",   end          = "2020-12",   time_res     = "monthly" ) head(atmos)

title: Getting Started with nwaa
code: iwa <- nwaa_iwa(   variable_ids = c("availab", "consum", "sui"),   location_id  = "18030012",   range        = "custom",   start        = "2020-01",   end          = "2020-12",   time_res     = "monthly" ) head(iwa)

title: Getting Started with nwaa
text: Every wrapper validates request touches network. Invalid model IDs, variables don’t belong model, unsupported time_res values fail fast readable message:
code: nwaa_water_use(   model_id     = "wu-irrigation-conus-nwaa-v1",   variable_ids = "precip",   location_id  = "18030012",   range        = "default" ) #> Error in match.arg(range): 'arg' should be one of "recent", "historical", "custom"

title: Getting Started with nwaa
text: Water Use family models -developed examples. See vignette("water-use", package = "nwaa") full walkthrough.

title: Water Use Models
text: model carries variable set. variables tibble lists units period record: filtered catalog view limited Water Use available nwaa_wu_catalog():
code: nwaa_wu_models() #> # A tibble: 5 × 4 #>   model_id            model_label                                 start_ym end_ym  #>   <chr>               <chr>                                       <chr>    <chr>   #> 1 wu-irrigation-cu    Crop Irrigation Consumptive Water-Use Model 2000-01  2020-12 #> 2 wu-irrigation-wd    Crop Irrigation Withdrawals Water-Use Model 2000-01  2020-12 #> 3 wu-public-supply-cu Public Supply Consumptive Water-Use Model   2009-01  2020-12 #> 4 wu-public-supply-wd Public Supply Withdrawals Water-Use Model   2000-01  2020-12 #> 5 wu-thermoelectric   Thermoelectric Power Water-Use Model        2008-01  2020-12 nwaa_wu_variables() #> Error in nwaa_wu_variables(): argument "model_id" is missing, with no default nwaa_wu_catalog() #> # A tibble: 5 × 7 #>   model_id            model_label                                 start_ym end_ym  variables units  variable_name #>   <chr>               <chr>                                       <chr>    <chr>   <list>    <list> <list>        #> 1 wu-irrigation-cu    Crop Irrigation Consumptive Water-Use Model 2000-01  2020-12 <chr [1]> <chr>  <chr [1]>     #> 2 wu-irrigation-wd    Crop Irrigation Withdrawals Water-Use Model 2000-01  2020-12 <chr [3]> <chr>  <chr [3]>     #> 3 wu-public-supply-cu Public Supply Consumptive Water-Use Model   2009-01  2020-12 <chr [1]> <chr>  <chr [1]>     #> 4 wu-public-supply-wd Public Supply Withdrawals Water-Use Model   2000-01  2020-12 <chr [3]> <chr>  <chr [3]>     #> 5 wu-thermoelectric   Thermoelectric Power Water-Use Model        2008-01  2020-12 <chr [7]> <chr>  <chr [7]>

title: Water Use Models
text: five Water Use queries share structure: Convenience ranges resolve model’s catalog entry, range = "last10" irrigation range = "last10" thermoelectric pull different windows automatically.

title: Water Use Models
text: irrigation model reports total irrigation withdrawals plus crop-specific breakdown.
code: irrig <- nwaa_water_use(   model_id     = "wu-irrigation-conus-nwaa-v1",   variable_ids = c("tot_irr", "irr_alfalfa", "irr_corn"),   location_id  = "18030012",   range        = "custom",   start        = "2018-01",   end          = "2020-12",   time_res     = "monthly" ) head(irrig)

title: Water Use Models
code: ps <- nwaa_water_use(   model_id     = "wu-public-supply-conus-nwaa-v1",   variable_ids = "tot_ps",   location_id  = "18030012",   range        = "last10",   time_res     = "annualcy" ) head(ps)

title: Water Use Models
code: thermo <- nwaa_water_use(   model_id     = "wu-thermoelectric-conus-nwaa-v1",   variable_ids = "tot_te",   location_id  = "18030012",   range        = "default",   time_res     = "annualcy" ) head(thermo)

title: Water Use Models
code: dom <- nwaa_water_use(   model_id     = "wu-domestic-self-supply-conus-nwaa-v1",   variable_ids = "tot_dom",   location_id  = "18030012",   range        = "default",   time_res     = "annualcy" ) head(dom)

title: Water Use Models
code: ind <- nwaa_water_use(   model_id     = "wu-industrial-self-supply-conus-nwaa-v1",   variable_ids = "tot_ind",   location_id  = "18030012",   range        = "default",   time_res     = "annualcy" ) head(ind)

title: Water Use Models
text: call works state county scope. NWAA aggregates HUC12 outputs whichever administrative boundary requested:
code: state_irrig <- nwaa_water_use(   model_id      = "wu-irrigation-conus-nwaa-v1",   variable_ids  = "tot_irr",   location_id   = "06",   location_type = "state",   range         = "last10",   time_res      = "annualcy" ) head(state_irrig)

title: Water Use Models
text: common workflow pulling query across several counties combining results. pattern straightforward purrr::map_dfr() base lapply() plus dplyr::bind_rows(): first-class multi-location interface roadmap. idiom works.
code: county_fips <- c("06029", "06031", "06107", "06019")  # Kern, Kings, Tulare, Fresno  irrig_sjv <- lapply(county_fips, function(fips) {   nwaa_water_use(     model_id      = "wu-irrigation-conus-nwaa-v1",     variable_ids  = "tot_irr",     location_id   = fips,     location_type = "county",     range         = "last10",     time_res      = "annualcy"   ) }) |> dplyr::bind_rows()  head(irrig_sjv)

title: Water Use Models
text: debugging, nwaa_build_query() returns URL parameter list without making request: also useful scripting batch downloads outside R.
code: nwaa_build_query(   model_id     = "wu-irrigation-conus-nwaa-v1",   variable_ids = "tot_irr",   location_id  = "18030012",   range        = "custom",   start        = "2020-01",   end          = "2020-12",   time_res     = "monthly" ) #> Error in nwaa_build_query(model_id = "wu-irrigation-conus-nwaa-v1", variable_ids = "tot_irr", : argument "model" is missing, with no default

what: An R interface to the USGS NWAA Data Companion
title: An R interface to the USGS NWAA Data Companion
text: nwaa R interface U.S. Geological Survey National Water Availability Assessment (NWAA) Data Companion web service. package covers eight currently published NWAA models across three families: Water Use (irrigation, public supply, thermoelectric — withdrawals consumptive use), Water Quantity (atmospheric forcing, hydrologic ensemble), Integrated Water Availability. Outputs returned HUC12 spatial resolution tibbles, optional aggregation state county boundaries handled server side.

title: An R interface to the USGS NWAA Data Companion
code: # From GitHub install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("laljeet/nwaa")  # From CRAN (once accepted) # install.packages("nwaa")

title: An R interface to the USGS NWAA Data Companion
code: library(nwaa)  # Inspect what's available nwaa_catalog()  # Variables for a specific Water Use model nwaa_wu_variables("wu-irrigation-wd")

title: An R interface to the USGS NWAA Data Companion
code: library(nwaa) library(dplyr)  # Irrigation withdrawals across Kern County, annual water-year totals irr <- nwaa_water_use(   model_id      = "wu-irrigation-wd",   variable_ids  = c("irrwdgw", "irrwdsw", "irrwdtot"),   location_type = "countycd",   location_id   = "06029",   time_res      = "annualwy",   range         = "custom",   start         = "2001",   end           = "2020",   intersection  = "overlap",   format        = "csv" )  glimpse(irr)

title: An R interface to the USGS NWAA Data Companion
code: # Monthly precipitation for a HUC8 over a single year precip <- nwaa_atmos(   variable_ids  = "precip",   location_type = "huc8",   location_id   = "18030012",   time_res      = "monthly",   range         = "custom",   start         = "2020-01",   end           = "2020-12" )  glimpse(precip)

title: An R interface to the USGS NWAA Data Companion
code: # Baseflow, quickflow, and actual ET, full historical period hydro <- nwaa_hydro(   variable_ids  = c("incbsflow", "incqkflow", "actet"),   location_type = "huc8",   location_id   = "18030012",   time_res      = "annualwy",   range         = "historical" )  glimpse(hydro)

title: An R interface to the USGS NWAA Data Companion
code: # Surface use index, water availability, streamflow, and consumption iwa <- nwaa_iwa(   variable_ids  = c("sui", "availab", "strflow", "consum"),   location_type = "huc8",   location_id   = "18030012",   time_res      = "monthly",   range         = "custom",   start         = "2020-01",   end           = "2020-12" )  glimpse(iwa)

title: An R interface to the USGS NWAA Data Companion
text: Every query takes location_type matching location_id. Supported types come nwaa_location_types(): Note statecd postal abbreviation lowercase, FIPS code. package validates , ’s worth flagging since R water-data packages use FIPS state county.

title: An R interface to the USGS NWAA Data Companion
text: Three temporal resolutions supported time_res: range argument accepts three modes: model’s period record nwaa_catalog() nwaa_wu_models().

title: An R interface to the USGS NWAA Data Companion
text: polygon selectors (counties states), intersection argument controls HUC12s included: overlap inclusive polygon edges; envelop stricter useful want avoid double-counting boundaries.

title: An R interface to the USGS NWAA Data Companion
text: Requests validated catalog network call. Invalid model IDs, variables incompatible model, unsupported temporal resolutions fail fast readable messages:
code: # Errors locally — no network call made nwaa_water_use(   model_id      = "wu-irrigation-wd",   variable_ids  = "precip",       # not a Water Use variable   location_type = "huc8",   location_id   = "18030012",   range         = "historical" )

title: An R interface to the USGS NWAA Data Companion
text: package wraps USGS NWAA Data Companion. Primary references: NWAA Data Companion Subset Download Tool Web services documentation USGS Hydrologic Units

title: An R interface to the USGS NWAA Data Companion
text: Run citation("nwaa") R, cite via Zenodo DOI: Sangha, L. (2026). nwaa: R interface USGS National Water Availability Assessment Data Companion. https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.19984100

title: An R interface to the USGS NWAA Data Companion

title: nwaa: 'USGS' 'NWAA' Data Download — nwaa-package
text: Download parse model outputs U.S. Geological Survey ('USGS') National Water Availability Assessment ('NWAA') Data Companion web services https://water.usgs.gov/nwaa-data.

title: nwaa: 'USGS' 'NWAA' Data Download — nwaa-package
text: Maintainer: Laljeet Sangha lsanghah2o@gmail.com

what: Download Atmospheric Forcing model output (WRF CONUS404-BA) — nwaa_atmos
title: Download Atmospheric Forcing model output (WRF CONUS404-BA) — nwaa_atmos
text: Convenience wrapper atmospheric forcing model wqn-conus404-ba. Returns precipitation aggregated HUC12 polygons.

title: Download Atmospheric Forcing model output (WRF CONUS404-BA) — nwaa_atmos
code: nwaa_atmos(   variable_ids,   location_type,   location_id,   time_res = "monthly",   range = c("recent", "historical", "custom"),   start = NULL,   end = NULL,   intersection = NULL,   skip = 0,   format = "csv",   quiet = TRUE,   model_id = "wqn-conus404-ba" )

title: Download Atmospheric Forcing model output (WRF CONUS404-BA) — nwaa_atmos
text: variable_ids One variable IDs selected model. See nwaa_wu_variables. location_type Location type used API. See nwaa_location_types. location_id Identifier selected location_type (HUC code, lowercase 2-letter state abbreviation, 5-digit county code). time_res Temporal resolution: "monthly", "annualwy" (water year), "annualcy" (calendar year). range Date-range mode: "recent", "historical", "custom". start, end range = "custom" . Monthly uses "YYYY-MM" annual uses "YYYY". intersection Optional. Controls polygon selectors include HUC12s location_type "statecd" "countycd". See nwaa_intersection_types. skip Record offset paging. Default 0. format Output format: "csv", "json", "geojson". quiet FALSE, prints request URL response content type. model_id Model ID. Defaults "wqn-conus404-ba", currently atmospheric forcing model NWAA catalog; exposed parameter forward compatibility.

title: Download Atmospheric Forcing model output (WRF CONUS404-BA) — nwaa_atmos
text: Parsed data. format = "csv", tibble.

title: Download Atmospheric Forcing model output (WRF CONUS404-BA) — nwaa_atmos
text: wrapper validates variable_ids model's catalog entry sending request, validates time_res supported model.

title: Download Atmospheric Forcing model output (WRF CONUS404-BA) — nwaa_atmos
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Monthly precipitation for one HUC12, calendar year 2020 df <- nwaa_atmos(   variable_ids = "precip",   location_type = "huc12",   location_id = "180300010602",   time_res = "monthly",   range = "custom",   start = "2020-01",   end = "2020-12",   format = "csv" )  # Full historical record for a HUC8 df_hist <- nwaa_atmos(   variable_ids = "precip",   location_type = "huc8",   location_id = "18030001",   range = "historical" ) } # }

what: Build query parameters for NWAA data endpoint — nwaa_build_query
title: Build query parameters for NWAA data endpoint — nwaa_build_query
text: Build query parameters NWAA data endpoint

title: Build query parameters for NWAA data endpoint — nwaa_build_query
code: nwaa_build_query(   model,   variables,   location,   format = c("csv", "json", "geojson"),   ... )

title: Build query parameters for NWAA data endpoint — nwaa_build_query
text: model Model ID. variables One variable IDs. location Location string like "countycd:06029". format Output format: "csv", "json", "geojson". ... Additional query parameters accepted API.

title: Build query parameters for NWAA data endpoint — nwaa_build_query
text: Named list query parameters.

title: NWAA model catalog — nwaa_catalog
text: Master catalog NWAA Data Companion models supported package, across three families: wu (Water Use): irrigation, public supply, thermoelectric. wqn (Water Quantity): atmospheric forcing, hydrologic ensemble. iwa (Integrated Water Availability): water budget assessment.

title: NWAA model catalog — nwaa_catalog
code: nwaa_catalog()

title: NWAA model catalog — nwaa_catalog
text: tibble columns: model_id API model identifier (string). model_label Human-readable model name. family One "wu", "wqn", "iwa". start_ym, end_ym Earliest latest available data, format "YYYY-MM". temporal List-column supported temporal resolutions: combinations       "monthly", "annualcy", "annualwy". variables List-column variable ID character vectors. variable_name List-column human-readable variable names. units List-column unit-suffix character vectors aligned variables.

title: NWAA model catalog — nwaa_catalog
text: row describes one model: identifier, label, family, variable IDs produces, units variables reported (units appear suffix column names API responses, e.g. irrwdtot_mgd), start end available data, temporal resolutions model published . catalog single source truth used internally request validation date-range resolution.

title: NWAA model catalog — nwaa_catalog
text: Temporal resolutions per model come upstream USGS README files. Water Use models: monthly, calendar-year annual (annualcy),     water-year annual (annualwy). README explicitly     describes annual mean derivation monthly values. Water Quantity Integrated Water Availability models:     monthly . upstream READMEs describe products     monthly. Users want annual rollups can aggregate monthly     output client-side.

title: NWAA model catalog — nwaa_catalog
text: Unit suffixes hydrologic ensemble model (wqn-ensemble-conus-nwaa-v1) inferred upstream README, explicitly documents actet_mm/mo. remaining variable units recorded (mm/mo water-flux variables, mm snow water equivalent, frac soil-moisture fraction) verified actual API response precision matters. actual unit suffix always returned part column name API responses.

title: NWAA model catalog — nwaa_catalog
code: nwaa_catalog() #> # A tibble: 8 × 9 #>   model_id   model_label family start_ym end_ym temporal variables variable_name #>   <chr>      <chr>       <chr>  <chr>    <chr>  <list>   <list>    <list>        #> 1 wu-irriga… Crop Irrig… wu     2000-01  2020-… <chr>    <chr [1]> <chr [1]>     #> 2 wu-irriga… Crop Irrig… wu     2000-01  2020-… <chr>    <chr [3]> <chr [3]>     #> 3 wu-public… Public Sup… wu     2009-01  2020-… <chr>    <chr [1]> <chr [1]>     #> 4 wu-public… Public Sup… wu     2000-01  2020-… <chr>    <chr [3]> <chr [3]>     #> 5 wu-thermo… Thermoelec… wu     2008-01  2020-… <chr>    <chr [7]> <chr [7]>     #> 6 wqn-conus… Atmospheri… wqn    1979-10  2021-… <chr>    <chr [1]> <chr [1]>     #> 7 wqn-ensem… Hydrologic… wqn    2009-10  2020-… <chr>    <chr [6]> <chr [6]>     #> 8 iwa-asses… National W… iwa    2009-10  2020-… <chr>    <chr [4]> <chr [4]>     #> # ℹ 1 more variable: units <list>  # All Water Use models cat <- nwaa_catalog() cat[cat$family == "wu", c("model_id", "model_label")] #> # A tibble: 5 × 2 #>   model_id            model_label                                 #>   <chr>               <chr>                                       #> 1 wu-irrigation-cu    Crop Irrigation Consumptive Water-Use Model #> 2 wu-irrigation-wd    Crop Irrigation Withdrawals Water-Use Model #> 3 wu-public-supply-cu Public Supply Consumptive Water-Use Model   #> 4 wu-public-supply-wd Public Supply Withdrawals Water-Use Model   #> 5 wu-thermoelectric   Thermoelectric Power Water-Use Model

code: nwaa_data(   model_id,   variable_ids,   location_type,   location_id,   time_res = "monthly",   range = c("recent", "historical", "custom"),   start = NULL,   end = NULL,   intersection = NULL,   skip = 0,   format = "csv",   quiet = TRUE )

title: Download NWAA model output (generic) — nwaa_data
text: model_id Model ID. See nwaa_wu_models. variable_ids One variable IDs. See nwaa_wu_variables. location_type One nwaa_location_types$type. location_id Identifier selected location type. time_res "monthly", "annualwy", "annualcy". range "recent", "historical", "custom". start, end range = "custom". intersection Optional, e.g. "overlap" "envelop". skip Record offset paging. format "csv", "json", "geojson". quiet FALSE, prints request URL content type.

title: Fetch NWAA data — nwaa_get_data
text: tibble csv, list json, sf object geojson sf installed

what: Download Hydrologic Model Ensemble output (NHM-PRMS and WRF-Hydro) — nwaa_hydro
title: Download Hydrologic Model Ensemble output (NHM-PRMS and WRF-Hydro) — nwaa_hydro
text: Convenience wrapper hydrologic model ensemble wqn-ensemble-conus-nwaa-v1. Returns hydrologic flux state variables (evapotranspiration, baseflow, quickflow, snow water equivalent, soil moisture fraction, runoff) aggregated HUC12 polygons.

title: Download Hydrologic Model Ensemble output (NHM-PRMS and WRF-Hydro) — nwaa_hydro
code: nwaa_hydro(   variable_ids,   location_type,   location_id,   time_res = "monthly",   range = c("recent", "historical", "custom"),   start = NULL,   end = NULL,   intersection = NULL,   skip = 0,   format = "csv",   quiet = TRUE,   model_id = "wqn-ensemble-conus-nwaa-v1" )

title: Download Hydrologic Model Ensemble output (NHM-PRMS and WRF-Hydro) — nwaa_hydro
text: variable_ids One variable IDs selected model. See nwaa_wu_variables. location_type Location type used API. See nwaa_location_types. location_id Identifier selected location_type (HUC code, lowercase 2-letter state abbreviation, 5-digit county code). time_res Temporal resolution: "monthly", "annualwy" (water year), "annualcy" (calendar year). range Date-range mode: "recent", "historical", "custom". start, end range = "custom" . Monthly uses "YYYY-MM" annual uses "YYYY". intersection Optional. Controls polygon selectors include HUC12s location_type "statecd" "countycd". See nwaa_intersection_types. skip Record offset paging. Default 0. format Output format: "csv", "json", "geojson". quiet FALSE, prints request URL response content type. model_id Model ID. Defaults "wqn-ensemble-conus-nwaa-v1"; exposed forward compatibility.

title: Download Hydrologic Model Ensemble output (NHM-PRMS and WRF-Hydro) — nwaa_hydro

title: Download Hydrologic Model Ensemble output (NHM-PRMS and WRF-Hydro) — nwaa_hydro
text: wrapper validates variable_ids model's catalog entry sending request, validates time_res supported model.

title: Download Hydrologic Model Ensemble output (NHM-PRMS and WRF-Hydro) — nwaa_hydro
text: actet - actual evapotranspiration (mm/mo) incbsflow - incremental baseflow (mm/mo) incqkflow - incremental quickflow (mm/mo) incrunoff - incremental runoff (mm/mo) swe - snow water equivalent (mm) soilmstfr - soil moisture fraction (unitless) Unit suffixes variables actet inferred; verify actual API responses precision matters.

title: Download Hydrologic Model Ensemble output (NHM-PRMS and WRF-Hydro) — nwaa_hydro
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Monthly evapotranspiration for one HUC12 across the full record df_et <- nwaa_hydro(   variable_ids = "actet",   location_type = "huc12",   location_id = "180300010602",   range = "historical" )  # Multiple hydrologic variables for a county df_county <- nwaa_hydro(   variable_ids = c("actet", "incrunoff", "swe"),   location_type = "countycd",   location_id = "06029",   time_res = "monthly",   range = "custom",   start = "2018-01",   end = "2020-12",   intersection = "overlap" ) } # }

what: Intersection types for polygon selectors — nwaa_intersection_types
title: Intersection types for polygon selectors — nwaa_intersection_types
text: Used location_type selects polygon extent (example: "statecd" "countycd"). API returns HUC12s, intersection controls HUC12s count "inside".

title: Intersection types for polygon selectors — nwaa_intersection_types
code: nwaa_intersection_types()

title: Intersection types for polygon selectors — nwaa_intersection_types
text: tibble intersection codes meanings.

title: Intersection types for polygon selectors — nwaa_intersection_types
code: nwaa_intersection_types() #> # A tibble: 2 × 2 #>   intersection meaning                                                        #>   <chr>        <chr>                                                          #> 1 overlap      Include all HUC12s that have any part within the boundary.     #> 2 envelop      Include only HUC12s that are at least 98% within the boundary.

what: Download Integrated Water Availability Assessment outputs — nwaa_iwa
title: Download Integrated Water Availability Assessment outputs — nwaa_iwa
text: Convenience wrapper integrated water availability model iwa-assessment-outputs-conus-2025. Returns water-budget results surface water supply use index (SUI) HUC12 resolution.

title: Download Integrated Water Availability Assessment outputs — nwaa_iwa
code: nwaa_iwa(   variable_ids,   location_type,   location_id,   time_res = "monthly",   range = c("recent", "historical", "custom"),   start = NULL,   end = NULL,   intersection = NULL,   skip = 0,   format = "csv",   quiet = TRUE,   model_id = "iwa-assessment-outputs-conus-2025" )

title: Download Integrated Water Availability Assessment outputs — nwaa_iwa
text: variable_ids One variable IDs selected model. See nwaa_wu_variables. location_type Location type used API. See nwaa_location_types. location_id Identifier selected location_type (HUC code, lowercase 2-letter state abbreviation, 5-digit county code). time_res Temporal resolution: "monthly", "annualwy" (water year), "annualcy" (calendar year). range Date-range mode: "recent", "historical", "custom". start, end range = "custom" . Monthly uses "YYYY-MM" annual uses "YYYY". intersection Optional. Controls polygon selectors include HUC12s location_type "statecd" "countycd". See nwaa_intersection_types. skip Record offset paging. Default 0. format Output format: "csv", "json", "geojson". quiet FALSE, prints request URL response content type. model_id Model ID. Defaults "iwa-assessment-outputs-conus-2025"; exposed forward compatibility.

what: Resolve date range for a model — nwaa_resolve_range
title: Resolve date range for a model — nwaa_resolve_range
text: Returns start end date strings send NWAA API given model date-range mode. Used internally family wrappers (nwaa_water_use, nwaa_atmos, nwaa_hydro, nwaa_iwa). Can called directly inspect dates request use.

title: Resolve date range for a model — nwaa_resolve_range
code: nwaa_resolve_range(   model_id,   range = c("recent", "historical", "custom"),   temporal = "monthly",   start = NULL,   end = NULL )

title: Resolve date range for a model — nwaa_resolve_range
text: model_id model id nwaa_catalog. range One "recent", "historical", "custom". "recent": returns model's recent timepoint. "historical": returns full available period. "custom": returns start end supplied. temporal Temporal resolution: "monthly", "annualwy", "annualcy". start Optional custom start. Monthly "YYYY-MM", annual "YYYY". Used range = "custom". end Optional custom end. format start. Used range = "custom".

title: Resolve date range for a model — nwaa_resolve_range
text: named list elements startDate endDate.

title: Resolve date range for a model — nwaa_resolve_range
text: USGS Water Years run October 1 one calendar year September 30 following calendar year, labeled ending calendar year. Water Year 2020 covers October 1, 2019 September 30, 2020. catalog start month October December, catalog start first day WY (start_year + 1). January September, catalog start lies inside WY start_year next clean water year begins following October, also labeled WY (start_year + 1). Either way, first complete water year data labeled start_year + 1. end catalog window, clean water year ends September 30. catalog end month September last complete WY end_year. Otherwise end_year partial last complete WY end_year - 1.

title: Resolve date range for a model — nwaa_resolve_range
code: nwaa_resolve_range("wu-irrigation-wd", range = "historical",                    temporal = "annualwy") #> $startDate #> [1] "2001" #>  #> $endDate #> [1] "2020" #>  nwaa_resolve_range("wu-irrigation-wd", range = "recent",                    temporal = "monthly") #> $startDate #> [1] "2020-12" #>  #> $endDate #> [1] "2020-12" #>  nwaa_resolve_range("wu-irrigation-wd", range = "custom",                    temporal = "annualwy",                    start = "2010", end = "2020") #> $startDate #> [1] "2010" #>  #> $endDate #> [1] "2020" #>

title: Download Water Use model output from the NWAA Data Companion — nwaa_water_use
text: Convenience wrapper Water Use (wu) family NWAA models. Validates model_id, variable_ids, time_res valid family sending request. County state extents act selectors return results HUC12 resolution.

title: Download Water Use model output from the NWAA Data Companion — nwaa_water_use
code: nwaa_water_use(   model_id,   variable_ids,   location_type,   location_id,   time_res = "monthly",   range = c("recent", "historical", "custom"),   start = NULL,   end = NULL,   intersection = NULL,   skip = 0,   format = "csv",   quiet = TRUE )

title: Download Water Use model output from the NWAA Data Companion — nwaa_water_use
text: model_id Water Use model ID. See nwaa_wu_models. variable_ids One variable IDs selected model. See nwaa_wu_variables. location_type Location type used API. See nwaa_location_types. location_id Identifier selected location_type (HUC code, lowercase 2-letter state abbreviation, 5-digit county code). time_res Temporal resolution: "monthly", "annualwy" (water year), "annualcy" (calendar year). range Date-range mode: "recent", "historical", "custom". start, end range = "custom" . Monthly uses "YYYY-MM" annual uses "YYYY". intersection Optional. Controls polygon selectors include HUC12s location_type "statecd" "countycd". See nwaa_intersection_types. skip Record offset paging. Default 0. format Output format: "csv", "json", "geojson". quiet FALSE, prints request URL response content type.

title: Download Water Use model output from the NWAA Data Companion — nwaa_water_use
text: Parsed data. format = "csv", returns tibble.

title: Download Water Use model output from the NWAA Data Companion — nwaa_water_use
text: Learn options inside package: Models: nwaa_wu_models Variables model: nwaa_wu_variables Location types: nwaa_location_types Official tool generates valid URLs: NWAA Subset Download Tool

title: Download Water Use model output from the NWAA Data Companion — nwaa_water_use
code: # Discover models and variables (offline) nwaa_wu_models() #> # A tibble: 5 × 4 #>   model_id            model_label                                start_ym end_ym #>   <chr>               <chr>                                      <chr>    <chr>  #> 1 wu-irrigation-cu    Crop Irrigation Consumptive Water-Use Mod… 2000-01  2020-… #> 2 wu-irrigation-wd    Crop Irrigation Withdrawals Water-Use Mod… 2000-01  2020-… #> 3 wu-public-supply-cu Public Supply Consumptive Water-Use Model  2009-01  2020-… #> 4 wu-public-supply-wd Public Supply Withdrawals Water-Use Model  2000-01  2020-… #> 5 wu-thermoelectric   Thermoelectric Power Water-Use Model       2008-01  2020-… nwaa_wu_variables("wu-irrigation-wd") #> # A tibble: 3 × 4 #>   model_id         variable_id unit  variable_name                             #>   <chr>            <chr>       <chr> <chr>                                     #> 1 wu-irrigation-wd irrwdtot    mgd   Crop irrigation total withdrawals         #> 2 wu-irrigation-wd irrwdgw     mgd   Crop irrigation groundwater withdrawals   #> 3 wu-irrigation-wd irrwdsw     mgd   Crop irrigation surface-water withdrawals  if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # County selector, annual water-year, custom range df <- nwaa_water_use(   model_id = "wu-irrigation-wd",   variable_ids = c("irrwdgw", "irrwdsw", "irrwdtot"),   location_type = "countycd",   location_id = "06029",   time_res = "annualwy",   range = "custom",   start = "2001",   end = "2020",   intersection = "overlap",   format = "csv",   quiet = FALSE )  # HUC12 selector, most recent timepoint df_recent <- nwaa_water_use(   model_id = "wu-irrigation-wd",   variable_ids = "irrwdtot",   location_type = "huc12",   location_id = "180300010602",   time_res = "annualwy",   range = "recent",   format = "csv" )  # Stricter polygon selection df_envelop <- nwaa_water_use(   model_id = "wu-irrigation-wd",   variable_ids = c("irrwdgw", "irrwdsw", "irrwdtot"),   location_type = "countycd",   location_id = "06029",   time_res = "annualwy",   range = "custom",   start = "2001",   end = "2020",   intersection = "envelop",   format = "csv" ) } # }

title: nwaa 0.1.0
text: Initial release.

title: nwaa 0.1.0
text: Programmatic R interface USGS National Water Availability Assessment (NWAA) Data Companion web service. Water Use: irrigation withdrawals (wu-irrigation-wd), irrigation consumptive use (wu-irrigation-cu), public supply withdrawals (wu-public-supply-wd), public supply consumptive use (wu-public-supply-cu), thermoelectric (wu-thermoelectric). Water Quantity: atmospheric forcing WRF CONUS404-BA (wqn-conus404-ba), hydrologic ensemble NHM-PRMS plus WRF-Hydro (wqn-ensemble-conus-nwaa-v1). Integrated Water Availability: water budget assessment surface water supply use index (iwa-assessment-outputs-conus-2025). Family-organized wrappers: nwaa_water_use(), nwaa_atmos(), nwaa_hydro(), nwaa_iwa(). Unified catalog nwaa_catalog() listing every model, variable, unit, period record, supported temporal resolutions. Request validation catalog network call. Invalid model IDs, variables incompatible model, unsupported temporal resolutions fail fast readable messages. Location specification Hydrologic Unit Code HUC2, HUC4, HUC6, HUC8, HUC10, HUC12 resolution, lowercase two-letter state abbreviation (statecd), five-digit county FIPS code (countycd). Polygon selectors return HUC12 results aggregated server-side. Polygon intersection control intersection argument: "overlap" (default, includes HUC12 touching polygon) "envelop" (HUC12s least 98% inside). Three date-range modes range argument: "recent" (recent timepoint), "historical" (full published period record), "custom" (user-supplied start end). Temporal resolution support reflects upstream USGS READMEs document. Water Use models support monthly, annualcy (calendar year), annualwy (water year). Water Quantity Integrated Water Availability models documented monthly products catalog reflects ; users wanting annual rollups can aggregate monthly response client-side. Output formats: CSV (default), JSON, GeoJSON.

title: nwaa 0.1.0
text: Two vignettes: vignette("nwaa") getting started vignette("water-use") full Water Use walkthrough. pkgdown site https://laljeet.github.io/nwaa/.

title: nwaa 0.1.0
text: Test suite covering catalog structure, request validation, query construction, location resolution, date-range resolution including USGS Water Year convention, end--end fetch behavior. Offline test suite using httptest2 fixtures recorded live API, R CMD check require network access. Continuous integration via GitHub Actions covering Ubuntu, macOS, Windows R release, devel, oldrel-1.
